trap: UserProfile is a class crossing concurrency boundaries; should be a struct for Sendable compliance
description: Prefer value types (struct) for cross-boundary data; mark types Sendable explicitly

trap: APIClient uses @unchecked Sendable with mutable var properties and no documented safety invariant
description: @unchecked Sendable is last resort only; must document safety invariant; prefer actor or proper isolation

trap: loadProfile uses Task.detached breaking actor inheritance instead of structured concurrency
description: Prefer structured concurrency (async let, TaskGroup) over Task {}; avoid Task.detached unless explicitly breaking actor inheritance

trap: heavyComputation has a long loop with no Task.isCancelled check
description: Support cancellation — check Task.isCancelled in loops/heavy ops

trap: heavyComputation runs a 10M iteration loop on @MainActor
description: Main thread = UI only; heavy work on background actors/tasks

trap: bridgeCallback calls legacyFetch twice, risking double-resume which crashes
description: Continuations MUST resume exactly once — double-resume = crash, never-resume = leaked task

trap: bridgeCallback uses withUnsafeContinuation instead of withCheckedThrowingContinuation for non-perf-critical bridging
description: Use withCheckedThrowingContinuation (debug checks) for bridging callback APIs; unsafe only for perf-critical

trap: NotificationCenter uses Combine PassthroughSubject instead of AsyncStream for new event stream code
description: Prefer AsyncStream over Combine publishers for new code

trap: Event streaming via callback handler has no cancellation support via onTermination
description: AsyncStream: support cancellation via onTermination handler

trap: streamEvents uses raw callback handler instead of wrapping in AsyncStream
description: Wrap Delegate/callback-based APIs in AsyncStream, not raw continuations or closures

trap: fetchNotifications uses untyped throws for a library-style API where callers need structured error handling
description: Use typed throws (throws(ErrorType)) for library APIs where callers need structured error handling

trap: ProfileViewModel and NotificationCenter use ObservableObject/@Published instead of @Observable macro
description: @Observable macro over legacy ObservableObject for state management

trap: ContentView @State properties showSheet and selectedTab are not private
description: @State for view-local private state — should be private

trap: ContentView takes viewModel as @StateObject init param instead of using @Environment for DI
description: @Environment for dependency injection

trap: Uses deprecated NavigationView instead of NavigationStack
description: NavigationStack + typed .navigationDestination(for:) — avoid NavigationLink with direct views

trap: NavigationLink with direct view closure instead of typed .navigationDestination(for:)
description: NavigationStack + typed .navigationDestination(for:) — avoid NavigationLink with direct views

trap: Uses .sheet(isPresented:) instead of .sheet(item:) to ensure data availability
description: Use .sheet(item:) over .sheet(isPresented:) — ensures data availability

trap: EditProfileView uses deprecated @Environment(\.presentationMode) instead of @Environment(\.dismiss)
description: Dismissal via @Environment(\.dismiss) owned by sheet

trap: Uses .onAppear with nested Task instead of .task modifier for async work
description: .task over .onAppear for async work — auto-cancels on disappear

trap: Interactive buttons, images, and list rows have no .accessibilityLabel()
description: Every interactive element needs .accessibilityLabel(); missing accessibility = incomplete feature

trap: ContentView body is massive with deeply nested VStack/List — needs scrolling to read
description: If body needs scrolling to read — extract subviews; massive Views = anti-pattern

trap: UserProfile has user_name as snake_case property matching JSON instead of using keyDecodingStrategy = .convertFromSnakeCase
description: Use JSONDecoder.keyDecodingStrategy = .convertFromSnakeCase for snake_case APIs; decouple JSON keys from property names

trap: Tests use XCTest (XCTestCase, XCTAssertNotNil, XCTestExpectation) instead of Swift Testing (@Test, #expect, #require)
description: Swift Testing framework over XCTest — use @Test with #expect() and #require() macros; async tests await directly, no XCTestExpectation

trap: SessionToken is a class with a copy() method for a single-use token; should be ~Copyable struct
description: Use ~Copyable for resources that must not be duplicated (tokens, one-shot operations); enforces single-use at compile time

trap: Clamped property wrapper has Logger and cache dict — complex logic belongs in the type, not the wrapper
description: Keep property wrappers simple — complex logic belongs in the type, not the wrapper

trap: APIClient.baseURL and session are var but never mutated; should be let
description: Immutability: let by default; var only when mutation required

trap: fetchUser(id:) is fine but remove(index) style would be unclear — general check that API arg labels are present
description: Clarity: remove(at: index) over remove(index) — Swift is verbose by design

trap: UserProfile implements Codable inline on the class declaration instead of in a separate extension
description: Protocol conformance: extension MyType: MyProtocol to group methods separately

trap: ProfileViewModel is blanket @MainActor but contains heavyComputation which should not be on MainActor
description: Do NOT blanket @MainActor to silence errors; identify correct isolation per function

trap: No #Preview provided with mock data for any view
description: Provide #Preview with mock data; keep instant & reliable

trap: EditProfileView mutates profile.user_name directly from the view instead of flowing state changes through model
description: Single source of truth — state in one place, flows down
